title: leontodon scopulorum
side_effects: Leontodon scopulorum can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, gastrointestinal issues, and skin irritation. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged use may lead to kidney damage and liver toxicity. Topical application of the plant may result in skin rashes, redness, and itching, and in severe cases, anaphylaxis. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using Leontodon scopulorum for any health-related purposes.
